stem: A retail store buys a jacket for $80 and sells it at a 40% markup on the purchase price. What is the selling price of the jacket?

distractorRationale.A: Student calculated only the markup amount ($80 × 0.40 = $32) and stopped without adding it to the cost.
distractorRationale.C: Student applied the markup to $100 instead of $80: $100 × 0.40 = $40, then $80 + $40 = $120.
distractorRationale.D: Student subtracted the markup instead of adding: $80 − $32 = $48.
